title: potentilla cinerea subsp. subacaulis
intro: Potentilla cinerea subsp. subacaulis, also known as dwarf cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the western coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
